allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking strengthens comparative-political-systems-analysis by focusing attention on institutional structure, incentives, and pattern interaction rather than isolated labels.
allies.source_quote: framework for seeing interrelationships rather than things

allies.rationale_text: Game-theory-payoffs supports comparative-political-systems-analysis by clarifying how different actors respond under different system rules and incentives.
allies.source_quote: Game theory thinking is the right tool to use in difficult competitive and adversarial settings

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias distorts comparative-political-systems-analysis when analysts interpret cross-system evidence to defend a preferred political frame.
antagonists.source_quote: motivated reasoning causes analysts to interpret cross-system evidence

structured_tensions.tension_text: Comparative-political-systems-analysis conflicts with first-principles-thinking when regime-specific precedent crowds out reconstruction from base institutional assumptions.
structured_tensions.source_quote: comparative-political-systems-analysis vs first-principles-thinking: regime-specific precedent crowds out reconstruction from base institutional assumptions.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Comparative-political-systems-analysis conflicts with confirmation-bias when motivated reasoning distorts cross-system evidence toward a preferred framework.
structured_tensions.source_quote: comparative-political-systems-analysis vs confirmation-bias: motivated reasoning causes analysts to interpret cross-system evidence in ways that confirm their preferred political framework.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Comparative-political-systems-analysis conflicts with status-quo-bias when inherited institutional arrangements are treated as default optimal instead of path-dependent.
structured_tensions.source_quote: comparative-political-systems-analysis vs status-quo-bias: inherited institutional arrangements are treated as default optimal rather than path-dependent outcomes.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
